entry.title: Unlimited OCR Works
entry.summary: Recently, end-to-end OCR models, exemplified by DeepSeek OCR, have once again thrust OCR into the spotlight. A widely held view is that employing a large language model (LLM) as the decoder allows the... \u00b7 Upvotes: 23 \u00b7 GitHub Stars: 5572 \u00b7 Authors: Youyang Yin, Huanhuan Liu, YY

entry.title: Looped World Models
entry.summary: Current world models face a fundamental tension: faithful long-horizon simulation demands deep computation, but deeper models are expensive to deploy and prone to compounding errors. We resolve this b... \u00b7 Upvotes: 457 \u00b7 Authors: Hongyuan Adam Lu, Z. L. Victor Wei, Qun Zhang

entry.title: ABot-Earth 0.5: Generative 3D Earth Model
entry.summary: We present ABot-Earth 0.5, a generative 3D framework designed to synthesize vast, seamless 3D environments from ubiquitous, geospatially referenced satellite imagery. To achieve this, we propose a nov... \u00b7 Upvotes: 481 \u00b7 GitHub Stars: 169 \u00b7 Authors: Ming Qian, Tianjian Ouyang, Mingchao Sun

entry.title: Agents' Last Exam
entry.summary: Recent AI systems have achieved strong results on a wide range of benchmarks, yet these gains have not translated into economically meaningful deployment across many professional domains. We argue tha... \u00b7 Upvotes: 361 \u00b7 GitHub Stars: 744 \u00b7 Authors: Yiyou Sun, Xinyang Han, Weichen Zhang

entry.title: PaddleOCR-VL-1.6: Expanding the Frontier of Document Parsing with Under-Optimized Region Refinement and Progressive Post-Training
entry.summary: We introduce PaddleOCR-VL-1.6, an upgraded compact document parsing model built upon PaddleOCR-VL-1.5. Although PaddleOCR-VL-1.5 establishes a strong 0.9B baseline, its remaining errors concentrate in... \u00b7 Upvotes: 22 \u00b7 GitHub Stars: 83669 \u00b7 Authors: Zelun Zhang, Hongen Liu, Suyin Liang

entry.title: Ultralytics YOLO26: Unified Real-Time End-to-End Vision Models
entry.summary: Real-time vision demands models that are accurate, efficient, and simple to deploy across diverse hardware. The YOLO family has become widely deployed for this reason, yet most YOLO detectors still re... \u00b7 Upvotes: 15 \u00b7 GitHub Stars: 58770 \u00b7 Authors: Glenn Jocher, Jing Qiu, Mengyu Liu

entry.title: Cosmos 3: Omnimodal World Models for Physical AI
entry.summary: We introduce Cosmos 3, a family of omnimodal world models designed to jointly process and generate language, image, video, audio, and action sequences within a unified mixture-of-transformers architec... \u00b7 Upvotes: 134 \u00b7 GitHub Stars: 10537 \u00b7 Authors: Aditi, Niket Agarwal, Arslan Ali

entry.title: COLLEAGUE.SKILL: Automated AI Skill Generation via Expert Knowledge Distillation
entry.summary: LLM agents are increasingly expected not only to complete isolated tasks, but also to carry bounded representations of human expertise, judgment, and interaction style. entry.title: Crafter: A Multi-Agent Harness for Editable Scientific Figure Generation from Diverse Inputs

entry.title: Gamma-World: Generative Multi-Agent World Modeling Beyond Two Players

entry.title: SkillOpt: Executive Strategy for Self-Evolving Agent Skills
entry.summary: Agent skills today are hand-crafted, generated one-shot, or evolved through loosely controlled self-revision, none of which behaves like a deep-learning optimizer for the skill, and none of which reli... \u00b7 Upvotes: 246 \u00b7 GitHub Stars: 9102 \u00b7 Authors: Yifan Yang, Ziyang Gong, Weiquan Huang
